title: 360° Orbit Around a Standoff
prompt: A smooth 360-degree orbit at constant height and distance around [SUBJECT] (or, if the scene has two figures in confrontation, around both), the surrounding space revealed as the camera rotates. 40mm, gimbal-smooth, no cuts. Motion is the pressure — tense, operatic. Low light and long shadows only if the scene's time allows.

title: Crash Zoom Discovery
prompt: Static wide of the scene, then a sudden crash zoom punches in on [SUBJECT] — a slight handheld instability after it settles, as if the operator just spotted them. 1970s documentary zoom feel, muted grade. The zoom itself is the discovery.

title: Handheld Follow Through a Crowd
prompt: Urgent handheld follows close behind [SUBJECT] as they push through the scene's space, camera at shoulder height, occasionally bumped, lens flaring off practicals. 28mm wide, shallow focus snapping between the subject and passing faces. Kinetic, immersive, anxious street-cinema energy. Single continuous take; grounded, stable physics.

title: Snorricam Spiral
prompt: Snorricam rig locked to [SUBJECT]'s chest as they move in panic — their face pinned steady in frame while the whole world lurches and spins behind them, lights smearing into streaks. 24mm wide, slightly low angle up at the face. Disorienting, subjective — a breakdown rendered in camera grammar.

title: Vertigo Dolly Zoom
prompt: Medium on [SUBJECT] as the camera performs a slow dolly zoom — pulling back while zooming in — so they stay the same size while the background stretches sickeningly behind them. The world elongates as realization hits their face. Queasy and unhurried; ambient sound thins to a ringing tone.

title: Gutter-Level Tracking
prompt: Camera skims an inch above the ground, tracking alongside [SUBJECT]'s feet as they walk, the surface below catching reflected light and breaking it with each step. 32mm at gutter level. Moody, tactile — the space seen from below, every footstep the beat.

title: Clean Over-the-Shoulder
prompt: Over-the-shoulder coverage: if the shot has a second character, frame past their shoulder and jawline (soft, out of focus, anchoring one third) onto [SUBJECT]'s face in clean focus, eyeline just off-lens. 50mm at chest height, soft key on the near face; cut to match its reverse. If the shot is a single, treat it as a clean 50mm single instead.

title: Insert: Hands Tell It
prompt: Insert: [SUBJECT]'s hands in close-up carrying the real storytelling — turning an object, hesitating over a choice, a small repeated motion. Shallow macro focus, light matched to the scene. Emotion located in the hands while the face holds its secret.

title: Reaction Single
prompt: Clean reaction single: [SUBJECT] framed chest-up against gentle bokeh, reacting to what was just said — the performance entirely in micro-shifts: a blink held too long, jaw tension, the breath before deciding not to speak. 75mm, eyeline close to lens. Hold longer than feels natural.

title: Rack Focus Two Truths
prompt: Static frame on [SUBJECT] sharp in the foreground. If the scene places a meaningful element or second character deeper in the shot, a deliberate rack focus trades the planes mid-beat — foreground melting, background snapping into evidence — a cut that happens inside one shot. 85mm wide open. If there is nothing meaningful behind, hold the single instead.

title: The Held Master
prompt: A single locked wide master that refuses to cut: the whole beat played out in real time at real distance, the scene's own geography and doorways doing the blocking. 32mm, practical light. Trust the distance and the performance; the audience edits with their eyes.

title: Mirror Without the Camera
prompt: The beat played through a mirror: faces visible mainly as reflections, the real bodies soft at frame's edge, blocking arranged so the camera never appears. When [SUBJECT] finally turns from the mirror to face directly, the shot cuts to reality for the first time — and the space feels colder there. 40mm.

title: Slow Zoom Lie Detector
prompt: A near-imperceptible zoom across a single answer: [SUBJECT] speaks, calm and credible, while the lens creeps from medium to close, the tightening frame doing the interrogation no one in the scene conducts. By the end the eyes fill the frame, and one blink lands where the truth would have a seam. 50-85mm.

title: The Walk-Away Hold
prompt: After the beat's last line, [SUBJECT] exits — and the camera does not follow. The locked 40mm frame holds on the emptied space, sound settling, footsteps fading. The hold runs past comfortable: absence given screen time. Cut only when the feeling has finished.

title: Kitchen Argument Two-Shot
prompt: If the shot has two characters mid-argument: static two-shot, both faces in focus, the distance between them as the shot — no cutting. One keeps at a small idle task and will not look up; their eyes meet once in a reflection and flick away. Long silence held in frame. 40mm, warm practical light if the scene allows. The scene's own lines carry it.

title: Interrogation Across the Table
prompt: Slow oscillating track behind the questioner's shoulder as they press [SUBJECT], who leans into the light and holds the stare a beat too long before answering, eyes flat and unhurried. Hard top-light carving the faces, surroundings falling to black if the scene allows. 50mm; tension carried in the pauses. Needs two characters and the scene's own questions and answers.

title: Whispered Confession in Church
prompt: Slow push toward [SUBJECT] from behind. They speak barely above breath without turning; the other, if one is present, simply nods once. The space swallows the words. 75mm, heads against a blurred background. Echoing stillness.

title: Doorway Breakup
prompt: If the scene has two characters, stage the beat on a threshold: one inside, one in the doorway, the doorframe splitting the frame and the light — warm interior against a colder beyond. Eyes to the floor, lifting only for the answer; a hand rests on the door's edge throughout. 40mm static. The close is a door, slow, no slam.

title: Shot/Reverse: Matching Close-Ups
prompt: Matching close-up coverage, 65mm, eyelines tight to the lens on both sides so the audience sits inside the conversation — [SUBJECT]'s eyes searching the other's face, the reverse catching the exact moment a defense fails: a blink-rate change, the mouth's small surrender before the words agree. Cut on the looks, not the lines. Backgrounds melted to nothing. Needs two characters.

title: Dirty Singles: The Negotiation
prompt: Negotiation coverage in dirty singles — each close-up keeps the rival's blurred shoulder in frame so neither face is ever alone. Watch for the tell: a held gaze while the hand stops moving; a small accommodating smile the eyes decline to join. Pauses negotiated as hard as the words. 75mm, reflective surroundings if the scene has them. Needs two characters.

title: Waiting Room, 3 AM
prompt: Locked two-shot of two characters waiting side by side under flat, unflattering light. Dialogue arrives in fragments with long gaps; eyes lift toward a door every time something moves, hope and dread the same face. One takes the other's hand without looking; the beat continues through the grip more than the words. 40mm. Needs two characters.

title: Monologue Push-In
prompt: Slow dolly-in on [SUBJECT] delivering a confession to an unseen listener off-frame, or into the space if none. The voice stays level but the hands betray them; the eyes hold, drop for the hardest phrase, and climb back after. The push lands at a tight close-up exactly as the last line ends. 65mm, shallow focus. Room tone only.

title: Silent Reaction Hold
prompt: Locked-off close-up of [SUBJECT] taking something in silently — a small exhale, a swallow, the eyes going back to re-read, the jaw setting. No dialogue. The shot holds well past comfortable. 85mm, soft single key with gentle falloff. Restraint over melodrama.

title: The Calm Explanation
prompt: Menace played quiet: a 50mm slow push-in that stops just past comfortable. [SUBJECT] speaks without raising their voice — eyes warm and unblinking, the threat living in how reasonable it sounds; a small genuine smile at their own logic; once, mid-sentence, the eyes go cold and snap back to warm so fast it is barely caught. Let the push do the pressure.

title: Single-Candle Chiaroscuro
prompt: Locked-off medium close-up of [SUBJECT] lit by a single candle, the flame the only source — deep Rembrandt shadows, warm flickering highlights on skin, the background falling to black. 85mm, very shallow focus; the flame trembles when they exhale. Painterly and quiet.

title: Fluorescent Office Dread
prompt: Slow push down an empty corridor lit by flat, cold fluorescent, one fixture strobing irregularly at the far end. Symmetrical one-point perspective, every door closed. The ballast hum is the only sound. 32mm, clinical sterile grade. Mounting unease without showing anything.

title: Neon Rain Close-Up
prompt: Tight slow-orbiting close-up of [SUBJECT]'s face at night, lit entirely by unseen cycling neon — magenta, cyan across the skin; droplets on the eyelashes in razor focus if rain fits the scene. 65mm, anamorphic oval bokeh of blurred signage. Saturated, romantic melancholy, time slowed by longing.

title: Moonlight Bedroom
prompt: Night interior in believable moonlight: one soft blue-white source raking through a window, sculpting [SUBJECT] in cool half-tones, everything else in readable darkness — not video-game blue, just cold and quiet. A passing car's headlights sweep the wall once and leave. 50mm slow push.

title: Phone-Lit Face
prompt: Close shot of [SUBJECT] lit solely by their phone — cold under-glow modeling the face from below, the light shifting as they scroll; the thing they stop on visible only as a change in the eyes and a brightness tick on the skin. The rest of the frame black. 65mm. Intimate, isolating.

title: Police Lights Through Curtains
prompt: Interior night: red and blue pulses wash through a window or curtains, sweeping the walls in alternation, no siren — silent lights painting the room in turns of alarm. [SUBJECT] at the window's edge, face strobing between the two colors and the dark, deciding whether tonight is about them. 50mm slow push.

title: Steam Room Shafts
prompt: Hard shafts of light dropping through dense steam or haze, figures as soft silhouettes appearing and dissolving in the white, sound mapping a space the eye cannot. The push drifts until [SUBJECT] resolves from the haze. 50mm. Light made physical, air you can grade.

title: Mirror Discipline
prompt: Locked shot framing [SUBJECT] and their reflection at a mirror. They lean down, the camera holds on the mirror, and when they rise everything is normal — they leave, and the reflection lingers one extra beat before following. No sting, no zoom, nothing underlined. 50mm, a single bare bulb. The scare planted quietly for whoever was paying attention.

title: Background Figure Focus
prompt: Static shot of [SUBJECT] sharp in the foreground, the space behind them a soft blur. The focus racks back: a pale figure stands deep in frame, perfectly still. Focus returns to [SUBJECT], unaware; when it racks back again, the space is empty. 85mm shallow depth as the weapon. The awful patience of the frame.

title: POV: Opening the Door
prompt: First-person POV: a hand enters frame low, turns a handle, and pushes — the space beyond revealed exactly as [SUBJECT] discovers it, the camera making a real head's small adjustments: a settle, a left-to-right scan, a held beat of hesitation, then a fix on the one detail that matters, the gaze tightening like a caught breath. 28mm, natural sway.

title: Waking in the Ward
prompt: First-person waking: black, then a slow blink-open — the ceiling soft, doubling, resolving. The POV's focus hunts like real waking eyes across the space, then finds a face leaning in from the side that takes three blinks to become someone known. Their relief breaks as the POV steadies on them. A hand squeezes, felt at frame's edge, and the eyes go glassy, the world swimming once more. 35mm POV.

title: Canvas and Count
prompt: POV of taking the blow that drops you: the world snaps sideways, lights wheeling overhead, the ground arriving huge, sound dropping into deep water. A face leans in upside-down, its words arriving on delay. The will to rise rendered visually — your own breath the loudest thing alive — and the frame steadies by force of will. 24mm POV. Single continuous take; grounded, stable physics.

title: Storm Manor
prompt: Establishing shot that introduces the scene's key building as a character with intentions: a slow low push toward it, the sky and weather taken from the scene, one lit window, small unsettling details along the approach. 32mm; first drops on the lens if rain fits.

title: The Lit Window
prompt: Night establishing of the scene's street or exterior — dark and still except one lit window; a slow push toward it, a silhouette crossing behind the curtain once. Ambient night sound. Everything asleep except whoever's story this is. 50mm.

title: College Quad, October
prompt: Aerial drift establishing a campus or open space: paths crossing a lawn, trees in their season shedding with each gust, a tower throwing a long shadow, bells striking the hour mid-shot. Institutional permanence in its prettiest month. 35mm aerial; leaf rustle and distant sound.

title: 360 One-Take Performance
prompt: Single-take music video: the camera orbits [ARTIST] performing while the world assembles around them in real time — lights flaring on as it passes, dancers stepping into formation, a band materializing piece by piece — until the final revolution reveals a full show in progress. 28mm continuous orbit synced to the track's build.

title: Strobe-Cut Performance
prompt: Performance built on strobe logic: pitch black, then a single strobe freezes [ARTIST] mid-move against a colored seamless — each flash a new pose, background color, cut exactly on the kick drum. The black between flashes carries the bass. Crisp fashion lighting in each frozen frame. 50mm. Relentless, graphic, kinetic.

title: Projector Skin Performance
prompt: Intimate performance against black: [ARTIST] in close-up while projected imagery plays across their face and shoulders — memories, rushing clouds, scrolling lyrics — the projection the only light source, conforming to every contour. The camera pushes slowly through the beam, catching dust in the cone. 65mm. Confessional.

title: Single Spotlight Ballad
prompt: Stripped ballad staging: [ARTIST] on a stool in a black void, one overhead spotlight, dust drifting in the cone. A slow 85mm orbit is the only motion through the first verse; at the bridge the spot dims almost to nothing, the silhouette carrying the climax, then blooms back for the final line. Voice, light, restraint.

title: Swinging Bulb
prompt: Performance lit by one swinging bulb: [ARTIST] stays planted while the bulb's arc drags light and shadow across them in rhythm — face appearing and vanishing on the downbeats, eyes catching the filament at each pass. The swing decays through the verse and is shoved back into motion for the chorus. 50mm. The performer as the constant.

title: Invisible Cut Through a Window
prompt: The camera pushes toward a lit window and passes seamlessly through the glass without a visible cut, continuing into the interior mid-scene, as if we were always inside. Cool exterior gives way to warm interior as it crosses the threshold; muffled outside sound swells into room audio at the pass-through. 32mm.

title: Dream Logic Dissolve Chain
prompt: A floating, slightly slow camera drifts through a chain of dissolves with dream logic — each space bleeding a motif into the next, [SUBJECT] appearing in each, always a little farther away. Soft diffusion, long dissolves overlapping movement, sound smearing between spaces like memory. 40mm.
